title: Two-body brittleness failure mode
summary: The chapter's negative case: without a third coordinating body (context, feedback, or meta-learner), a two-body system — rules/data, robot/program, data/model, policy/execution — cannot adapt to novelty and degrades when conditions shift.
source: Ch03 — "When Two Bodies Aren't Enough" (MYCIN, industrial robots, supervised learning, bureaucracy)

nodes.label: Novel Conditions
nodes.description: Distribution shift, edge cases, new context

edges.description: Rules applied to data produce output
Novelty exposes gaps the system can't handle
A third body would loosen rigid rules
A third body would absorb novelty before failure

identifiability_note: The missing third body is a latent confounder on both the rule-application path and the brittleness outcome; you cannot estimate 'how much worse two-body is' without constructing the three-body counterfactual.
